claim: The 2016 Lancet Breastfeeding Series estimated that universal breastfeeding could prevent 820000 child deaths annually, making it the single most effective child-survival intervention.

source.title: Breastfeeding in the 21st century: epidemiology, mechanisms, and lifelong effect

method: Reviewed Lancet series on breastfeeding epidemiology, survival benefits, and intervention effectiveness.
limitations: Mortality reduction estimates are modeled and depend on baseline mortality assumptions.
Intervention-effectiveness data is strongest for low-income settings.

claim: UNICEF Global Breastfeeding Scorecard reports 44 percent early initiation and 48 percent exclusive breastfeeding at six months globally, with the lowest rates in West and Central Africa and South Asia.
method: Reviewed UNICEF scorecard, country-level breastfeeding indicators, and policy tracking.
limitations: Country-level data masks substantial sub-national variation.
Exclusive-breastfeeding data is self-reported and may have recall bias.
